meta.title: Jiangsu
meta.subtitle: Industry-cluster overview
meta.tagline: Jiangsu is China's manufacturing heavyweight — laptops and chips in the south, construction machinery and ships in the north, and the country's largest textile cluster in between. This is the cluster map of the province.
meta.methodology: Coordinates are from Google Places, converted from China's GCJ-02 datum to WGS-84 to sit correctly on the OpenStreetMap / CARTO basemap. Each pin marks a place's dominant manufacturing specialty drawn from current sourcing guides; clusters are indicative, not exclusive, and shift over time. The map covers Jiangsu's thirteen prefecture-level cities; the Yangtze River Delta overview maps Jiangsu within its wider region.

clusters.note: Electronics, semiconductors and precision manufacturing; a biopharma hub and the FDI showcase.
Laptop and electronics powerhouse — long made a large share of the world's notebooks (Foxconn, Compal); China's top county economy.
Garment city — one of China's largest clothing markets; children's wear and down jackets.
Steel, chemicals and a Yangtze deep-water port; woollen sweaters.
One of China's biggest silk and synthetic-fabric clusters and markets.
Germany's manufacturing base in China — 500+ German firms; precision machinery and a port.
Integrated circuits (SK Hynix), IoT, solar (Suntech) and precision machinery.
Textiles and metallurgy; menswear (HLA/Hailan) and major private industrials.
Purple-clay (Zisha) teapot capital; environmental-protection equipment and cables.
Engineering machinery, rail transit and graphene; solar (Trina) and batteries (CATL).
Laminate-flooring capital; nearby Hutang weaving and denim.
Provincial capital — electronics, software, petrochemicals, autos and steel.
Chemicals, paper, aerospace composites and auto parts; Zhenjiang vinegar.
The world's optical-lens capital — eyeglass lenses and frames.
Home-textile capital — the world's largest bedding and home-textile base and market; over half of China's bed linen.
Shipbuilding and marine engineering; fitness equipment and construction.
Plush-toy capital; gifts, packaging and photovoltaics.
World capital of toothbrushes and hotel amenities.
China Medical City — biopharma, vaccines and medical devices (Gaogang).
China's largest private shipbuilding base, on the Yangtze.
Auto base (Dongfeng Yueda Kia) and wind-power and new-energy equipment.
Construction-machinery capital — XCMG, China's largest; energy, silicon and woodworking.
Plywood and engineered-board capital; electrochemicals.
Food processing and salt chemicals; electronics and IT-enabled services.
Innovative-pharma hub (Hengrui, Kanion); a deep-water port and petrochemicals.
Crystal and quartz capital — natural and synthetic crystal, silica and gem crafts.
Yanghe baijiu, a top-three Chinese liquor; glass, textiles and e-commerce.

orientation.v: Suzhou and Wuxi: electronics and ICs (SIP, Kunshan, Wuxi), Taicang's German machinery, Yixing teapots — China's richest manufacturing belt.
Shengze silk, Changshu garments, Jiangyin wool, Zhangjiagang sweaters, Nantong home textiles — the country's largest textile cluster.
Xuzhou's XCMG construction machinery, Changzhou rail and solar, Nantong and Jingjiang shipbuilding, Nanjing petrochemicals.
Taizhou Medical City and Lianyungang's Hengrui; specialist towns for toothbrushes (Hangji), toys (Yangzhou), optical lenses (Danyang) and crystal (Donghai).
Yancheng autos, Suqian's Yanghe liquor, Huai'an food, Pizhou plywood — the rising centre and north.

notes: Jiangsu is China's manufacturing heavyweight — higher-tech and more capital-intensive than Zhejiang's small-private clusters, from semiconductors and laptops in the south to construction machinery and shipbuilding.

The wealthy Sunan south (Suzhou, Wuxi, Changzhou) runs on electronics, precision machinery and foreign investment; it also holds the country's largest textile cluster (Shengze, Changshu, Jiangyin, Nantong).
Alongside the heavy industry sit classic single-product towns: toothbrushes in Hangji, plush toys in Yangzhou, optical lenses in Danyang, crystal in Donghai, teapots in Yixing.

A pin marks a place's dominant specialty, not its only one; the big cities make many things. The Yangtze River Delta overview maps Jiangsu alongside Shanghai and Zhejiang; this map zooms in on the province.
